decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch, and Incineroar cannot effectively pressure it. Switching to Calyrex-Ice allows me to pivot into a bulky threat that resists Iron Hands' coverage and forces the opponent to respect a powerful STAB response.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and provides the best damage output against Iron Hands. Since Iron Hands cannot OHKO Calyrex-Ice, attacking is the optimal play to maintain momentum.

High Horsepower is a 2HKO and provides the most consistent damage output against Iron Hands. Since Iron Hands is running Leftovers instead of Assault Vest, it is less bulky than anticipated, and I can secure the KO within two turns while taking minimal damage.
Iron Hands is at 15% HP, meaning Glacial Lance is a guaranteed KO regardless of its leftovers recovery. Attacking now secures the KO and removes the threat.
Calyrex-Ice is at 22% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz or even Knock Off. Switching to Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check the physical threat.

Since both Incineroars resist each other's primary attacks and are in a speed tie, Parting Shot is the best way to gain momentum, lower the opponent's stats, and bring in a fresh check like Kingambit safely.
Incineroar is at 39% and risks being KO'd by a Flare Blitz or chipped down further; switching to Kingambit provides a safe resist to the opponent's STAB moves and allows me to maintain momentum.

Kingambit is threatened by a 2HKO from Flare Blitz and cannot effectively damage Incineroar. Switching to my own Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check their physical attackers.

Incineroar is fainted, so I must send in a replacement. Kingambit is the best defensive pivot here as it resists the opponent's likely Flare Blitz and Knock Off, and can threaten back with Sucker Punch or Kowtow Cleave.

Kingambit is threatened by a 2HKO from Flare Blitz and cannot KO Incineroar in return. Switching to Dragonite provides a bulky pivot that resists Fire-type moves, allowing me to preserve Kingambit for later while scouting the opponent's next move.

Calyrex-Ice is at 22% HP and will be KOed by any of Urshifu's attacks, especially Surging Strikes. Switching to Kingambit allows me to tank the hit and threaten Urshifu while preserving my remaining team members.
Kingambit is fainted, so I must send in a replacement. Flutter Mane is the best choice to outspeed and threaten Urshifu-Rapid-Strike with super-effective STAB or high neutral damage.

Calyrex-Ice is at 22% and will be KOed by Urshifu's Close Combat next turn, while dealing negligible damage in return. Switching to Dragonite preserves the HP for a potential late-game trade or setup opportunity.
Calyrex-Ice is at 3% HP and will be KOed by any move from Urshifu-Rapid-Strike. Switching to Dragonite preserves the potential for a revenge kill or defensive utility.
